key_insight: NebulaExp shows that a fully transparent, ablation-driven post-training pipeline can improve both instruction-following and reasoning performance on an 8B LLM without sacrificing reproducibility.
engineering_relevance: The paper offers a practical, low-data recipe for building domain-tuned LLMs, with explicit data filtering, difficulty grading, and teacher-based optimization that can guide lightweight deployment workflows.
method: Built two post-training branches on Qwen3-8B-base: an instruction model and a reasoning-specialized model
Curation pipeline used 3.84M SFT samples and 200K RL candidates with distillation, cross-verification, grading, and diversity sampling
Compared supervised fine-tuning, GRPO RL, and OPD/MOPD teacher-based training under full ablation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gains from 55.01 to 60.99 after SFT and to 61.85 after GRPO for the instruct branch, and from 73.88 to 75.17 for the reasoning branch, using benchmark and verifier-based evaluations.
watch_item: Results are reported for an 8B-scale base model and specific curated datasets, so transfer to other model sizes or domains may require retuning the data filters and teacher/verifier setup.
paper_title: NebulaExp-8B: An Empirical Post-Training Pipeline via Full-Scale Ablation Research
